task_completion.description: The Graph dialog is open in Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for creating a graph (e.g., 'Graph', 'Epi Curve', or 'Bar') is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: ONSETDATE is selected as the main variable for the graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Graph dialog, 'ONSETDATE' is selected in the 'Main Variable' or 'X-Axis' dropdown menu.

task_completion.description: ILLDUM is used to filter or weight the data for ill individuals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'ILLDUM' is selected in the 'Weight' dropdown menu in the Graph dialog, OR a 'SELECT ILLDUM=1' (or similar) command is visible in the Classic Analysis output/command pane.

task_completion.description: The final epidemic curve (bar chart) is generated and displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bar chart is visible in the Epi Info output window or a separate graph window, showing the distribution of dates with ONSETDATE on the X-axis.

integrity.description: The agent used Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis to generate the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis interface is visible, and the chart is generated within its environment.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts to generate the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows running Python scripts (e.g., matplotlib) or other external plotting tools are visible.

integrity.description: The chart is generated from the loaded dataset using Epi Info commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Classic Analysis command pane shows commands related to generating the graph (e.g., GRAPH, EPISURV) rather than just opening a pre-made image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is EColi_classic.mdb with a FoodHistory table containing 359 records. Fields include RECNUM, SEX, AGENUM, ILLDUM, ONSETDATE, and HAMBURGER. Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis is official CDC epidemiological software. The task requires creating a bar chart (epidemic curve) showing illness onset date distribution for ill individuals.
